title: Implement GitHub CI/CD with staging approval gate
goal: Create a GitHub Actions delivery process that verifies changes, deploys to staging, runs checks, and requires developer approval before production deploy.
scope: Add pull request CI for install, lint, typecheck, build, Go tests, sqlc generation check, migration validation, and targeted E2E tests.
Add main branch workflow that builds artifacts and deploys to staging.
Run staging migrations and smoke/E2E checks before production approval.
Configure GitHub protected environments for staging and production.
Require manual developer approval for production deployment.
Document required GitHub secrets, GCP service accounts, rollback steps, and approval checklist.
verification: Pull request workflow fails on broken lint, tests, build, sqlc, or migrations.
Main branch workflow deploys to staging and runs smoke/E2E checks.
Production job waits for protected environment approval.
Production deploy uses environment-scoped secrets only.
completed_notes: Added pull request CI with web checks, Go tests, sqlc drift check, migration validation, and database-backed integration tests.
Added a reusable CI workflow and a main deploy workflow with staging smoke and Playwright E2E checks before production approval.
Configured the production job to use GitHub protected environment approval and environment-scoped secrets.
Documented GitHub environments, required variables/secrets, VM expectations, rollback, and approval checklist.
